description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a spiny lobster, featuring a robust, segmented exoskeleton with a reddish-brown coloration and lighter orange highlights on the legs and tail. The body is elongated, with a broad, fan-shaped tail and a cephalothorax covered in small spines and nodules. Long, slender antennae extend from the head, curving outward and backward, while multiple pairs of walking legs are visible along the sides. The dorsal surface is dotted with white spots and rough textures, and the underside is smoother and lighter in color. The eyes are small and positioned on short stalks at the front of the head, and no large claws are present, distinguishing it from true lobsters.
description: A reddish-brown spiny lobster with a segmented, spiny exoskeleton, long antennae, and multiple walking legs, lacking large claws.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the lobster, highlighting the segmented body, long antennae, and detailed legs.
description_view_1: Top-down view emphasizing the broad, fan-shaped tail, symmetrical leg arrangement, and the curvature of the antennae.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with clear visibility of the tail and the spiny texture along the back.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the head, antennae extending forward, and the arrangement of the eyes and mouthparts.
